warning_findings: Pre-existing authenticated existence oracle on POST /api/blog/:postIdOrSlug/comments: resolvePostId (blog.controller.ts:352-360) uses any-status findById and createComment (blog.service.ts:377-384) returns 403 for existing non-public posts vs 404 for nonexistent ones, letting an authenticated member with a known UUID confirm existence of draft/unpublished/future-scheduled posts. Not introduced by subtask-4; recommend planner align to 404 or record formal acceptance.
test_sufficiency_summary: Four dedicated security-regression tests cover the predicate shape and the future-scheduled, genuinely-public, and draft cases for findPublishedById; findPublished/findPublishedBySlug assertions were tightened to require the publishedAt constraint.

Residual gaps (reaffirmed verifier NOTEs): the LessThanOrEqual operator type is not asserted (only key presence), and no controller-level test pins the listComments slug-then-id fallback wiring. Both are low risk; coverage is sufficient for this change's risk level.
documentation_sufficiency_summary: docs/README.md line 249 accurately documents both lookup paths, the full public-visibility predicate, and the 404 behavior for draft/unpublished/future-scheduled posts, consistent with every other public surface.
The POST route's 403-vs-404 contract is documented at lines 253/269; WARNING 1 asks the planner to confirm or change that contract.
